name: credits
whyMelodyRhythm: Mixed note values create a natural, improvisational feel.
whyMelodyDensity: Medium density keeps the melody active without being overwhelming.
whyCountermelody: Supporting harmony adds depth to the melodic phrases.

bass.whyInstrument: Simple triangle waves provide a clean, pop-style bass presence.

countermelody.whyInstrument: A light lead tone creates an answering hook without overpowering the main melody.

chords.whyInstrument: Electric piano tones provide a classic pop/jazz texture.

melody.whyInstrument: A clear lead sound ensures the melody is the focus.

drums.whyInstrument: Natural drum sounds provide a grounded rhythmic foundation.

whyBassInstrument: Simple triangle waves provide a clean, pop-style bass presence.
whyChordsInstrument: Electric piano tones provide a classic pop/jazz texture.
whyMelodyInstrument: A clear lead sound ensures the melody is the focus.
whyDrumsInstrument: Natural drum sounds provide a grounded rhythmic foundation.
whyMeter: Standard 4/4 meter for a driving pop anthem.
whyKey: F Major is a common key for this progression.
whyBpm: 124 BPM is a standard tempo for upbeat pop tracks.
whyMode: Ionian (Major) provides the bright, optimistic feel of the Royal Road.
whyProgression: The "Royal Road" progression (IV-V-iii-vi) is a staple of emotional J-Pop and Anime music.
whyBassPattern: Steady pulses keep the energy moving forward.
whyVoicing: Add 9 chords add a modern, lush color to the standard progression.
whyGroove: Straight timing keeps the rhythm formal and disciplined.
whyMelodyStyle: Stepwise motion creates a singing, lyrical line.
whyArrangement: Building up the arrangement creates a sense of progression and energy.
whyPalette: A mix of electronic and acoustic sounds for a modern pop feel.
whyArticulation: Standard articulation provides a clear, rhythmic bounce.
